text: The operation began with a takedown of the inferior pulmonary ligament.
In part, this allows for emergent division of the inferior pulmonary vein in the case of bleeding.
Station 9 lymph nodes were then dissected out and sent for frozen section analysis.
All sample lymph node stations were sent for frozen section.
The dissection then proceeds medially to clear off the inferior pulmonary vein.
The lung is retracted anteriorly and the inferior pulmonary vein is further dissected.
Level 7 and level 10L lymph nodes are dissected and sent for pathology.
Here you can see the pulmonary artery being dissected from a posterior approach.
The left upper lobe is then retracted inferiorly and level 5 lymph nodes are dissected.
After the lymphadenectomy was performed, attention was turned to the fissure.
Gentle pressure on the fissure led to identification of the pulmonary artery.
The interlobar pulmonary artery was dissected down to the plane of LaRiche.
The plane was continued posteriorly until a tunnel was completed to connect to the previously dissected posterior plane.
A partial stapling of the fissure improved the ability to identify and dissect branches of the pulmonary artery.
Dissection right along the pulmonary artery improves safety by allowing improved visualization of arterial branches.
Station 13 segmental lymph nodes are dissected.
The anterior aspect of the fissure was further dissected at the vessel sealer.

The segmental arteries were dissected out, including the posterior apical artery, lingular artery, superior segmental artery, anterior basilar artery, posterior basilar artery, and lateral basilar artery.
Here you can clearly see the different segmental arteries.
A six centimeter endo leader is used to facilitate safe stapling of bronchovascular structures.
The lateral basilar artery was then divided with a 45 millimeter white load stapler.
Immediately posterior to the lateral basilar artery is the lateral basilar bronchus.
Bronchial anatomy was confirmed bronchoscopically at the start of the case.
The segmental bronchus is grasped and retracted to facilitate dissection.
Bronchoscopic confirmation during the dissection was used.
The segmental bronchus is divided with a blue load stapler.
Blue load cartridges are used for division of segmental bronchi and green load are used for division of lobar bronchi.
The bronchial stump is grasped and retracted.
Posterior to it is the lateral basilar vein, which is identified and dissected.
The anterior basilar vein and posterior basilar vein were also identified and preserved.
The lateral basilar vein was small, so the decision was made to clip the vein and divide it with a vessel sealer.
The lateral basilar vein was then clipped with two clips proximally and one clip distally.
A vessel sealer is used to divide the vein.
ICG was reconstituted in 10 cc's of saline and 4 cc's of ICG was injected into the patient.
This clearly delineated the S9 segment.
The borders were marked to provide a clear staple line for the parenchymal dissection, which is the most challenging portion of these cases.
Here you can see the outline of the S9 segment on the lung parenchyma.
A series of blue load staplers were then used to perform the segmentectomy.
Parenchymal division is the most challenging aspect of a lateral basilar segmentectomy.
The vessel sealer was used to divide the parenchyma surrounding the hilum.
Use of the vessel sealer near the hilum allows for a geometrically complex parenchymal division.
When the segmentectomy was completed, Surgicel was placed for hemostasis and the specimen was removed with an endocatch bag through the anterior port.
The specimen was delivered interoperatively for frozen section analysis, which demonstrated a 7 millimeter margin near the anterior basilar segment.
Therefore, an additional wedger section was performed along the anterior basilar segment in order to augment the margin.
The wedge resection was removed in a large endo-catch bag.
Pro-gel was administered as we do with all lung resections to decrease risk of post-operative air leaks with focus on the hilum and the lung that was divided with the vessel sealer.
A 24 French chest tube was placed through the anterior port and the operation was concluded.
